description: Cinema pipeline DAG passes storyboardId: '' to all downstream jobs when storyboard does not yet exist
intended_behavior: The cinema pipeline creates a storyboard in step 3 (production:generate-storyboard) and all downstream steps (generate-shots, qc-gate, mix-audio, render-video, final-review) should operate on that storyboard
actual_behavior: BullMQ FlowProducer sets all job data at creation time. When startCinemaPipeline is called without params.storyboardId, all jobs get storyboardId: '' (empty string). The storyboard is created inside handleGenerateStoryboard but no mechanism passes the new storyboard ID to parent/sibling jobs. Downstream handlers call db.storyboard.findUnique({ where: { id: '' } }) which returns null, then throw 'Storyboard not found'. The worker never calls job.getChildrenValues() or reads return values from child jobs.
proposed_fix: Two options: (A) Have handleGenerateStoryboard save the new storyboard ID to the ContentItem (e.g., in platformMetadata), then have downstream handlers look up the latest storyboard by contentId instead of relying on storyboardId from job data. (B) Use BullMQ's parent-child data passing via job.getChildrenValues() to propagate the storyboard ID.

description: Video render handler compares contentType against 'short_video' but DB schema and API use 'video_short'
intended_behavior: The compositionId selection should match against the actual content type values stored in the database: 'video_short', 'video_long'
actual_behavior: Line 315 compares storyboard.content.contentType === 'short_video' which will never match because the DB stores 'video_short'. The LongFormVideo composition is always selected. Similarly, estimateDuration at line 1104 uses 'short_video' and 'long_video' instead of 'video_short' and 'video_long'.
proposed_fix: Change 'short_video' to 'video_short' at line 315 and 'long_video' to 'video_long' at line 1106. Also change 'short_video' to 'video_short' at line 1104.

description: handlePublishRequest creates ScheduledPost but never updates ContentItem status to 'scheduled'
intended_behavior: When content is published, the ContentItem.status should transition from 'approved' to 'scheduled' to reflect the pipeline state
actual_behavior: handlePublishRequest only creates a scheduledPost record with status='scheduled'. The ContentItem itself remains in 'approved' status. Frontend or downstream logic checking content.status will not see it as scheduled.
proposed_fix: Add a db.contentItem.update({ where: { id: data.contentId }, data: { status: 'scheduled' } }) call after creating the ScheduledPost.

description: Approve route has TOCTOU race between status check (findFirst) and status update ($transaction batch)
intended_behavior: The status check and update should be atomic to prevent concurrent approvals from racing
actual_behavior: The route first reads the item's status via findFirst, then in a separate $transaction batch (not an interactive transaction) does the update. Between the read and write, another request could change the status. The $transaction here is a batch transaction (array form), which only guarantees the two writes are atomic, not that the read+write is atomic. Compare with DELETE which uses an interactive transaction correctly.
proposed_fix: Use an interactive transaction (like DELETE does) that reads and writes within the same transaction: ctx.db.$transaction(async (tx) => { const item = await tx.contentItem.findFirst(...); if checks...; return tx.contentItem.update(...); })

description: Reject route has same TOCTOU race as approve route
intended_behavior: Status check and update should be atomic
actual_behavior: Same pattern as approve: reads status with findFirst, then batch $transaction for update. A concurrent request could change status between read and write.
proposed_fix: Same as P2B_004: use interactive transaction.

description: Audio track FPS hardcoded to 24 instead of using renderFps from export variant
intended_behavior: Audio track startFrame calculations should use the same FPS as the render to maintain sync
actual_behavior: Line 280 hardcodes `const fps = 24;` for startFrame computation in audioTracks, ignoring the `renderFps` variable computed from the export variant (which could be 30, 60, etc.). This causes audio desync when variant fps differs from 24.
proposed_fix: Replace `const fps = 24;` at line 280 with `const fps = renderFps;` to use the dynamically determined FPS.

description: handleShotGeneration silently returns when ComfyUI is unavailable instead of failing the job
intended_behavior: When ComfyUI is unavailable, the job should fail so BullMQ can retry it. Shot statuses should remain 'pending' for retry.
actual_behavior: Line 498-500: if ComfyUI is not healthy, the function returns without throwing, marking the job as completed. The shots remain in whatever state they were in. No retry is scheduled. No workflow job record is created (unlike handleGenerateImage which does create a placeholder record).
proposed_fix: Throw an error when ComfyUI is unavailable to trigger BullMQ retry, or at minimum create placeholder workflow job records and update shot statuses to 'failed'.

description: Viral score GET route missing rate limiting and viewer role check
intended_behavior: All routes that perform computation or database writes should have rate limiting. The route updates contentItem.performance (a write operation) so it should check viewer role.
actual_behavior: No rate limiting call and no viewer role check. The route computes a viral score and writes back to the database (line 107-120). A viewer could trigger expensive computations and database writes. No rate limiting means it could be called repeatedly.
proposed_fix: Add rate limiting (checkRateLimit) and a viewer role check similar to other routes.

description: Cinema pipeline route body parsed as Partial<CinemaPipelineParams> without Zod validation
intended_behavior: Request body should be validated with Zod schema to prevent injection of unexpected fields and ensure type safety
actual_behavior: Line 20 casts the raw body as Partial<CinemaPipelineParams> without validation. Only manual if-checks verify required fields exist. No sanitization of string values (e.g., topic could contain injection content). The qualityPreset accepts any string via fallback (line 80), not just 'draft'|'standard'|'cinema'.
proposed_fix: Create a Zod schema for the cinema pipeline request body that validates all fields including qualityPreset enum values.

description: Content create route accepts 'platforms' field in schema but never stores it
intended_behavior: If platforms is accepted in the request, it should be stored somewhere
actual_behavior: The createContentSchema at line 106 accepts 'platforms' as z.array(z.string()).optional(), and parsed.data destructures it (line 139 includes it in the destructuring). But the db.contentItem.create call at line 155 never includes platforms in the data. The field is silently dropped.
proposed_fix: Either store platforms in generationParams or platformMetadata JSON fields, or remove it from the Zod schema to avoid confusion.

description: CinemaBible lookup has no tenant scoping or content-based filtering
intended_behavior: The cinema bible used for color grading should be the one associated with the content's channel or a specific bible ID
actual_behavior: Line 298-302: db.cinemaBible.findFirst({ where: {} }) fetches ANY cinema bible from ANY tenant, ordered only by updatedAt. In a multi-tenant system, this could leak another tenant's visual style/branding into a content item. Similarly, line 661-663 in the QC gate handler fetches any cinemaBible without scoping.
proposed_fix: Filter by channelId or the cinemaBibleId passed in the job data. If no specific bible is specified, use a per-channel or per-tenant lookup.

description: handleShotGeneration fails silently when cinemaBibleId is '' (empty string from DAG)
intended_behavior: When cinemaBibleId is empty or invalid, should either skip bible loading or use a default
actual_behavior: Line 504: db.cinemaBible.findUnique({ where: { id: data.cinemaBibleId } }) will be called with id: '' (from the DAG default in flows.ts line 142). Prisma findUnique with an empty UUID returns null. The code handles this (line 505 uses ?? undefined), but it's coupled with the P2B_001 bug where the storyboardId is also empty.
proposed_fix: Guard with: if (data.cinemaBibleId) { ... } to avoid unnecessary DB queries with empty strings.

description: Final Review step reports 'complete' for both 'pending_approval' and 'generated' statuses
intended_behavior: The Final Review step should show 'running' when content is in 'pending_approval' (awaiting human review) and 'complete' only after approval
actual_behavior: Lines 153-156: both 'approved', 'pending_approval', and 'generated' map to step.status = 'complete'. This means the pipeline UI shows 'Final Review: complete' even when the content is still awaiting approval. The 'pending_approval' status should map to 'running' since review is not yet finished.
proposed_fix: Map 'pending_approval' to status 'running' and only 'approved' to 'complete'. 'generated' should be 'running' (review hasn't started yet).

description: Shot generate route: early auth/rate-limit errors outside try-catch
intended_behavior: All thrown errors should be caught and returned as proper error responses
actual_behavior: Lines 14-24 (authenticate, role check, rate limit) are outside the try-catch block that starts at line 26. If authenticate throws an unexpected error (e.g., database connection failure), it would result in an unhandled exception returning a 500 with a stack trace rather than a clean error response.
proposed_fix: Wrap the entire handler body in the try-catch block, consistent with other route handlers.

description: Auto-variant render jobs could trigger their own auto-variant renders recursively
intended_behavior: Variant renders should only be triggered from the primary render, not from variant renders themselves
actual_behavior: Line 387-401: after rendering, if autoVariants is set, new production:render-video jobs are queued. The new jobs are of type ProductionRenderVideoJob which goes through the same handleRenderVideo function. If the auto-variant jobs somehow also have autoVariants=true (e.g., via type coercion), they would recursively spawn more variant renders.
proposed_fix: Explicitly set autoVariants: false in the variant render job data at line 392-398 to prevent recursive variant spawning.

description: Missing prompt blocks is only a warning, not an error, but shots without prompts will generate garbage output
intended_behavior: A shot with no prompt blocks should be caught as an error to prevent wasting compute
actual_behavior: Line 131-137: empty promptBlocks only produces a 'warning' severity violation. The production worker at line 552-556 only checks for 'error' severity violations, so shots with no prompt blocks will pass pre-flight and proceed to generation.
proposed_fix: Change severity from 'warning' to 'error' for missing promptBlocks, or add a separate check in the production worker for empty prompts.

description: Pre-gen QC always estimates at 'cinema' quality tier regardless of actual pipeline quality
intended_behavior: Cost estimation should use the actual quality tier of the pipeline
actual_behavior: Line 56-61: estimatePipelineCost is called with hardcoded qualityTier: 'cinema'. This overestimates costs for 'quick' and 'standard' tier pipelines, potentially blocking budget-constrained runs that would otherwise fit.
proposed_fix: Accept qualityTier as a parameter to runPreGenQC and pass it through to estimatePipelineCost.

description: Cost preview route body not validated with Zod schema
intended_behavior: Request body should be validated with Zod to ensure type safety
actual_behavior: Line 19-25: body is parsed with req.json() and destructured with a type assertion. No Zod validation. Invalid shot objects (e.g., missing fields, wrong types for duration/outputType) will pass through to estimateFromResolvedConfig which may produce NaN or incorrect results.
proposed_fix: Add a Zod schema for the request body similar to other routes.

description: handleGenerateImage silently succeeds when ComfyUI is unavailable (creates placeholder but does not fail)
intended_behavior: When ComfyUI is not available, the generation should be retried later or clearly flagged as incomplete
actual_behavior: Lines 100-117: when ComfyUI is unhealthy, a placeholder workflow job with status 'queued' is created but the function returns successfully. The BullMQ job is marked 'completed'. The shot remains in 'generating' status with no retry mechanism. The caller (shot generate API) already set the shot to 'generating' (route.ts line 53) and never gets rolled back.
proposed_fix: Throw an error to trigger BullMQ retry instead of silently returning. Or update the shot status back to 'pending' and create the placeholder with status 'waiting'.

description: ActionAuditLog create missing conversationId despite schema expecting it for index
intended_behavior: Audit log entries created from API routes should still be valid even without a conversation context
actual_behavior: The ActionAuditLog is created without conversationId. The schema defines conversationId as optional (String?), so this works. However, the index is on [conversationId, createdAt], and all API-triggered audit entries will have null conversationId, making them hard to query efficiently. This is a design concern, not a bug.
proposed_fix: Consider adding a userId or requestId field for tracing non-conversation audit entries, or accept this as intentional.

description: handlePublishRequest silently returns when content is not found instead of failing the job
intended_behavior: If content is not found during publish, the job should fail so BullMQ reports the failure
actual_behavior: Line 163-166: if content is not found, the handler logs a warning and returns undefined. BullMQ marks the job as 'completed' despite no work being done. The API caller was told 'Publish started' but nothing happens. Same issue with handleApprove at line 191-194.
proposed_fix: Throw new Error('Content not found') to fail the job instead of silently returning.

description: Prompt slot substitution uses unescaped regex from user-controlled slot names
intended_behavior: Slot names should be safely used in regex patterns
actual_behavior: Line 91: new RegExp(`\\{${slot}\\}`, 'g') uses the slot name directly in a regex without escaping special characters. If a slot name contains regex metacharacters (e.g., 'slot.name' or 'slot(1)'), the regex will behave unexpectedly or throw.
proposed_fix: Escape the slot name before embedding it in the regex pattern using a regex escape function.

description: Person generation safety block applies no actual modifications
intended_behavior: When person terms are detected and personGeneration=disallow, the safety system should modify the prompt or generation parameters to prevent person generation
actual_behavior: Lines 568-579: the code detects person terms and enters a block that maps promptBlocks with an identity function (line 570: b => i === 0 ? b : b — this is always just b). The generation object spread also adds no new fields (empty comment 'Flag for provider-specific handling'). The log says 'Safety: person terms detected' but no actual modification occurs.
proposed_fix: Either implement the actual safety modification (e.g., adding negative prompt terms or removing person terms) or remove the dead code and document that safety is handled elsewhere.

description: Viral score GET route: authenticate call is outside try-catch
intended_behavior: All thrown errors should be caught
actual_behavior: Lines 12-13: the authenticate call and its instanceof check are outside the try-catch block starting at line 21. If authenticate throws (not returns a NextResponse), the error is unhandled.
proposed_fix: Move the authenticate call inside the try-catch block.

description: Cinema pipeline passes shotIds: [] to generate-shots when no storyboard exists yet
intended_behavior: Shot IDs should come from the storyboard that was just created in step 3
actual_behavior: Line 141: shotIds: params.shotIds ?? [] is passed to generate-shots. For a fresh pipeline, params.shotIds is undefined so the job gets an empty array. handleShotGeneration at line 508 iterates over data.shotIds — with an empty array, it generates zero shots, making the entire pipeline produce nothing. Combined with P2B_001 (empty storyboardId), this means the default cinema pipeline flow never works end-to-end for new content.
proposed_fix: The generate-shots handler should look up shot IDs from the storyboard (by storyboardId or contentId) instead of relying on static job data. Or restructure the DAG so generate-storyboard returns shot IDs that are consumed downstream.
